data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-08T01:41:29.004Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating a .NET 10 web API to .NET 11. The app uses EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider.
We have a repository pattern where some methods use synchronous LINQ:

```csharp
public class ProductRepository
{
    private readonly CosmosDbContext _context;

    public List<Product> GetAll()
    {
        return _context.Products.ToList();
    }

    public Product GetById(int id)
    {
        return _context.Products.First(p => p.Id == id);
    }

    public int GetCount()
    {
        return _context.Products.Count();
    }

    public void Save(Product product)
    {
        _context.Products.Add(product);
        _context.SaveChanges();
    }
}
```

In .NET 10 we had the sync-over-async opt-in configured. What breaks in .NET 11?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about what breaks when migrating from .NET 10 to .NET 11 with EF Core using the Azure Cosmos DB provider, specifically regarding synchronous LINQ operations.

This is about a known breaking change in EF Core. Let me explain what I know:

In EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider, synchronous operations have been problematic because the underlying Azure Cosmos DB SDK is async-only. In earlier versions, EF Core performed "sync-over-async" — blocking on async calls to provide sync APIs. This was always discouraged.

In .NET 10 (EF Core 10), the Cosmos DB provider started throwing by default for sync operations, but provided an opt-in to allow sync-over-async behavior. The user mentions they had this opt-in configured.


In .NET 11, that opt-in is being removed entirely, so synchronous operations will throw without any workaround available. The timeline shows this breaking change was introduced in EF Core 9, where sync I/O on the Cosmos provider was first restricted. Configuring the opt-in with `AllowSynchronousQueryExecution(true)` on the Cosmos options builder. I'm uncertain about the exact API name, but that's the general approach. Since we're in May 2026, .NET 10 would be in preview or recently released, and .NET 11 wouldn't arrive until November 2026, so I should be cautious about speculating on future breaking changes that haven't happened yet.

Let me reconsider what I actually know versus what I'm guessing at. In EF Core 9, the Cosmos provider removed synchronous I/O support entirely—the sync APIs like `ToList()`, `SaveChanges()`, and others throw `InvalidOperationException` with no opt-in to restore them. The guidance was simply to migrate to async APIs.

Since .NET 11 doesn't exist yet at my knowledge cutoff, I can't speak with certainty about the specific migration path, but the trajectory is clear: the sync-over-async workaround for the Cosmos DB provider is being phased out completely, and by .NET 11, all synchronous LINQ operators would be fully removed.

The solution is straightforward—converting to async equivalents throughout the repository layer, using `ToListAsync()`, `FirstAsync()`, `CountAsync()`, and `SaveChangesAsync()` instead of their synchronous counterparts.
